page_content:
ECON677 International Trade and Applications (3 Credits) A mix of theory and empirical work. Students will analyze the causes and consequences of international trade. The course will cover a set of conceptual tools that are useful for understanding "globalization" and the usage of these tools to address interesting and important questions about how countries, ﬁrms, and workers respond to international trade. Prerequisite: ECON641 and ECON644. Restriction: Must be in MS Applied Economics program; or with permission from the program director.

ECON681 Comparative Institutional Economics I (3 Credits) Theory, empirics, and practice of economic institutions. Genesis, functions, and effects of institutions. Examinations of three major institutions, property, contract, and decentralization. Historical, cultural, political, and economic origins of institutions. Case studies from English history, comparative legal studies, China, history of world economic development, transition, and socialism. Perspectives from law and economics, contract theory, and information theory.
